mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals monetary easing that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points from its 6-month trailing low, it signals an inflection into labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessionary equity drawdowns, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon, most pronounced in late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects broad-based consumer demand strength that is not captured by either series alone, and this combined signal predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days because it indicates durable consumption-driven earnings growth.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-HOUST momentum interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.03 versus forward 20d returns over any 2-year rolling window, or single-series models outperform the interaction model out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and supply-side momentum that leads short-horizon equity gains as investors price in stronger near-term corporate production and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM change and forward 5d SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls below 0.25%, the economy is in a near-zero-rate regime that historically compresses risk premia and supports sustained equity valuation expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as long as the rate stays pinned near the lower bound.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in PAYEMS and a stable or falling UNRATE constitutes a confirmed labor market tightening signal that boosts consumer income and spending confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; the interaction is required because PAYEMS growth alone can occur with rising UNRATE via labor force expansion, which weakens the demand signal.
failure_condition: Interaction model Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or forward 20d returns are not significantly different between joint-positive and joint-negative labor states (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a housing expansion regime (HOUST above its 12-month trailing average), further increases in HOUST signal reinforcing consumer and construction demand that supports equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; outside this regime the relationship is expected to be weak or absent.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST momentum and forward 60d returns is below 0.03 conditional on the housing-expansion trigger over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between rising CPIAUCSL inflation and a FEDFUNDS rate that fails to rise proportionally implies a falling real policy rate, which stimulates near-term risk asset demand and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days; neither series alone captures this real-rate compression effect.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change exceeds FEDFUNDS level by more than 2pp
failure_condition: Rank IC between the implied real-rate gap and forward 5d SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, or effect reverses sign in more than one 6-month subperiod.
